commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR centralizes npm registry URL resolution for OpenClaw update metadata checks, adds env and npmrc/userconfig handling, routes package-target fetches through it, and adds regression tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows the package-target update metadata helper hardcodes the public npm registry, and startup/status/update preflight callers flow through that helper; this review used source inspection rather than a live update run.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can remove the unused import that currently fails `check-lint`; no product or security judgment is needed.

target.title: fix(update): honor npm registry config in update checks
target.author: hclsys

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: hclsys
commands.command: re-review ŌĆö p2 addressed. **finding:** `npm_config_userconfig_registry` is not a real npm env key; resolver missed npmrc-backed registry config. **fix (head: a319085fc6):** - removed the bogus `npm_config_userconfig_registry` env key from `npm_registry_env_keys` - added `parsenpmrcregistry(content)` ŌĆö parses `registry=<url>` lines from npmrc file content, skipping comments - added `readnpmrcregistryfromfile(filepath)` ŌĆö reads an npmrc file, returns parsed registry url or null - added `resolvenpmregistrybaseurlasync(env)` ŌĆö checks env vars first; if default, reads npmrc at path from `npm_config_userconfig` / `npm_config_userconfig` (or `~/.npmrc` as fallback) - added `resolvenpmpackagetargetregistryurlasync(params)` ŌĆö async variant for use from async callers - wired `fetchnpmpackagetargetstatus()` through `resolvenpmpackagetargetregistryurlasync` so all update checks respect npmrc registry **precedence:** `npm_config_registry` ŌåÆ `npm_config_registry` ŌåÆ npmrc file at `npm_config_userconfig` / `npm_config_userconfig` ŌåÆ `~/.npmrc` ŌåÆ default **tests added (59/59 update-channels pass, 97/97 update-startup+update-cli pass):** - reads registry from `npm_config_userconfig` npmrc file - reads registry from `npm_config_userconfig` npmrc file - env var registry takes precedence over npmrc file - falls back to default when npmrc missing or has no registry key - `resolvenpmpackagetargetregistryurlasync` uses npmrc registry
commands.freeform_prompt: re-review ŌĆö P2 addressed. **Finding:** `npm_config_userconfig_registry` is not a real npm env key; resolver missed npmrc-backed registry config. **Fix (head: a319085fc6):** - Removed the bogus `npm_config_userconfig_registry` env key from `NPM_REGISTRY_ENV_KEYS` - Added `parseNpmrcRegistry(content)` ŌĆö parses `registry=<url>` lines from npmrc file content, skipping comments - Added `readNpmrcRegistryFromFile(filePath)` ŌĆö reads an npmrc file, returns parsed registry URL or null - Added `resolveNpmRegistryBaseUrlAsync(env)` ŌĆö checks env vars first; if default, reads npmrc at path from `npm_config_userconfig` / `NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG` (or `~/.npmrc` as fallback) - Added `resolveNpmPackageTargetRegistryUrlAsync(params)` ŌĆö async variant for use from async callers - Wired `fetchNpmPackageTargetStatus()` through `resolveNpmPackageTargetRegistryUrlAsync` so all update checks respect npmrc registry **Precedence:** `npm_config_registry` ŌåÆ `NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY` ŌåÆ npmrc file at `npm_config_userconfig` / `NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG` ŌåÆ `~/.npmrc` ŌåÆ default **Tests added (59/59 update-channels pass, 97/97 update-startup+update-cli pass):** - reads registry from `npm_config_userconfig` npmrc file - reads registry from `NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG` npmrc file - env var registry takes precedence over npmrc file - falls back to default when npmrc missing or has no registry key - `resolveNpmPackageTargetRegistryUrlAsync` uses npmrc registry
